sections.label: Navigation Bar
sections.instruction: This appears at the very top of every page. It shows your name and a button visitors can click to contact you.

fields.name: name
fields.label: Your Name or Brand
fields.instruction: This is the name shown in the top-left corner of your site. Use your real name or a short brand name.

fields.name: ctaLabel
fields.label: Top Button Text
fields.instruction: The text on the button at the top-right. Keep it short — like 'Hire Me', 'Let's Talk', or 'Get in Touch'.

sections.label: Introduction — First Thing Visitors See
sections.instruction: This is the opening screen of your portfolio. Your name is shown large and bold, with a short description of who you are. First impressions matter — keep it clear and confident.

fields.name: name
fields.label: Your Full Name
fields.instruction: Type your full name. This will appear as the biggest text on the page.

fields.name: title
fields.label: Your Job Title or Role
fields.instruction: What do you do? For example: Frontend Developer, Data Scientist, UX Designer, or Software Engineer.

fields.name: bio
fields.label: A Short Bio About Yourself
fields.placeholder: I build clean, fast web products that people love using. 4 years of experience working with startups and agencies across 3 continents.
fields.instruction: Write 2 to 3 sentences about yourself. What do you do, how long have you been doing it, and what makes you different? Write like you are talking to a real person — no need to be overly formal.

fields.name: avatar
fields.label: Your Profile Photo
fields.instruction: Upload a clear photo of yourself. A headshot with a plain background works best. This helps visitors connect with you.

fields.name: location
fields.label: Where You Are Based
fields.instruction: Type your city and country. For example: Berlin, Germany. Visitors and employers often want to know where you are located.

fields.name: availableForWork
fields.label: Are You Available for Work?
fields.instruction: Turn this ON if you are currently looking for jobs, freelance projects, or new opportunities. A small 'Available' badge will appear on your profile.

fields.name: resumeLink
fields.label: Link to Your Resume
fields.instruction: If your resume is saved online (for example on Google Drive), paste the link here. Make sure the file is set to 'Anyone with the link can view' before sharing.

sections.label: Skills
sections.instruction: List everything you know how to do — grouped into neat categories. For example, one group for programming languages, one for design tools, one for frameworks. Add as many groups as you need.

fields.name: title
fields.label: Skill Category Name
fields.instruction: Give this group a name. For example: Languages, Frameworks, Tools, Design, Cloud, or Databases.

fields.name: skills
fields.label: Skills in This Category
fields.instruction: Type each skill and press Enter to add it to the list. Add as many as you like.

sections.label: Work Experience
sections.instruction: Add the jobs, internships, or freelance work you have done. If you have no work experience yet, that is completely fine — just turn this section off using the toggle below.

fields.name: showExperience
fields.label: Show Work Experience on My Portfolio?
fields.instruction: Keep this ON if you have work experience to display. Switch it OFF if you have not worked yet and want to hide this section.

fields.name: role
fields.label: Your Job Title
fields.instruction: What was your official title at this job? For example: Software Engineer, Intern, or Freelance Developer.

fields.name: company
fields.label: Company or Organisation Name
fields.instruction: The name of the company or organisation you worked at.

fields.name: period
fields.label: When Did You Work There?
fields.instruction: Enter the start and end dates. If you still work there, write 'Present' as the end date. Example: Jan 2022 – Present.

fields.name: location
fields.label: Location
fields.instruction: Where was this job? You can write a city, a country, or just 'Remote' if you worked from home.

fields.name: description
fields.label: What Did You Do?
fields.placeholder: Built and maintained the company's main dashboard. Improved page load times by 45% and reduced bugs by introducing automated tests.
fields.instruction: Describe your key responsibilities and any results you are proud of. Try to mention real numbers if you can — for example: 'Reduced load time by 40%'. Keep it to 2 or 3 sentences.

sections.label: Projects
sections.instruction: This is the most important section of your portfolio. Add the best things you have built — personal projects, university work, freelance work, or anything you are proud of. Employers look here first.

fields.name: title
fields.label: Project Name
fields.instruction: What is this project called?

fields.name: date
fields.label: When Did You Build It?
fields.instruction: Type the year or month you completed it. For example: 2024 or March 2024.

fields.name: description
fields.label: What Does This Project Do?
fields.placeholder: A personal finance app that helps users track income and expenses. Built with React and Firebase, used by 300+ people.
fields.instruction: Describe your project in plain language. What problem does it solve? Who uses it? Mention any impressive numbers if you have them. Write 2 to 3 sentences.

fields.name: image
fields.label: Project Screenshot
fields.instruction: Upload a screenshot or preview image of your project. Pick the most visually appealing view of your project — this is what visitors see first.

fields.name: tags
fields.label: Technologies Used
fields.instruction: List the main tools and technologies used. Type each one and press Enter.

fields.name: github
fields.label: GitHub Code Link
fields.instruction: Paste the GitHub link where visitors can read your code. Leave this empty if the project is private.

fields.name: webapp
fields.label: Live Website Link
fields.instruction: If your project is live on the internet, paste the link here so visitors can try it out.

fields.name: showMembers
fields.label: Did Others Work on This With You?
fields.instruction: Turn this ON if this was a group or team project. You will be able to add your teammates' names below.

fields.name: name
fields.label: Team Member's Name
fields.instruction: Type the full name of this team member.

fields.name: github
fields.label: Their GitHub Link
fields.instruction: Paste their GitHub link so visitors can find them. This is optional.

sections.label: Education
sections.instruction: Add your academic background. Include your university, college, or any formal degree or diploma. You can also add online bootcamps here if they involved a structured learning programme.

fields.name: degree
fields.label: Degree or Qualification
fields.instruction: What did you study or earn? For example: Bachelor of Computer Science, A-Levels in Maths and Physics, or Diploma in Graphic Design.

fields.name: institution
fields.label: School or University Name
fields.instruction: Type the full name of the school, college, or university.

fields.name: period
fields.label: Years Attended
fields.instruction: When did you start and finish? If you are still studying, write your expected year of completion.

fields.name: grade
fields.label: Grade or GPA (Optional)
fields.instruction: Add your grade if you want to show it. For example: First Class Honours, 3.8 GPA, or Distinction. Leave this empty if you prefer not to share it.

fields.name: description
fields.label: Anything Worth Highlighting? (Optional)
fields.placeholder: Focused on machine learning and distributed systems. Graduated top of class.
fields.instruction: Add any extra detail you want visitors to know — like key subjects, your thesis topic, or an achievement. This is completely optional.

sections.label: Certifications
sections.instruction: Add any certificates you have earned from online courses or professional programmes — for example from Coursera, Udemy, Google, AWS, or Microsoft. If you have none, turn this section off below.

fields.name: showCertifications
fields.label: Show Certifications on My Portfolio?
fields.instruction: Keep this ON to display your certificates. Switch it OFF to hide this section if you have none yet.

fields.name: name
fields.label: Certificate or Course Name
fields.instruction: Type the full name of the certificate exactly as it appears on the certificate.

fields.name: issuer
fields.label: Issued By
fields.instruction: Who gave you this certificate? For example: Google, AWS, Udemy, or Meta.

fields.name: date
fields.label: Date Completed
fields.instruction: Type the month and year you completed this. For example: August 2023.

fields.name: link
fields.label: Verification Link (Optional)
fields.instruction: If your certificate has a link that proves it is real, paste it here. Visitors can click it to verify your certificate. This is optional but builds trust.

sections.label: Contact Information
sections.instruction: How should people reach you? Add your email and any social profile links you are comfortable sharing. Employers and clients will use these to get in touch.

fields.name: email
fields.label: Your Email Address
fields.instruction: Enter the email address you want people to contact you on. This is the most important contact detail — make sure it is one you check regularly.

fields.name: github
fields.label: Your GitHub Profile Link
fields.instruction: Paste the link to your GitHub profile. Employers will click this to look at your code. For example: https://github.com/yourname.

fields.name: linkedin
fields.label: Your LinkedIn Profile Link
fields.instruction: Paste your LinkedIn profile link. LinkedIn is the most popular platform for professional networking and job searching.

fields.name: twitter
fields.label: Your Twitter / X Profile Link
fields.instruction: Paste your Twitter or X profile link if you use it professionally. Leave this empty if you do not.

fields.name: showTwitter
fields.label: Show Twitter / X Link?
fields.instruction: Turn this ON to show your Twitter link on your portfolio.

fields.name: facebook
fields.label: Your Facebook Profile Link
fields.instruction: Paste your Facebook profile link if you want to include it.

fields.name: showFacebook
fields.label: Show Facebook Link?
fields.instruction: Turn this ON to show your Facebook link on your portfolio.

fields.name: instagram
fields.label: Your Instagram Profile Link
fields.instruction: Paste your Instagram profile link if you want to include it.

fields.name: showInstagram
fields.label: Show Instagram Link?
fields.instruction: Turn this ON to show your Instagram link on your portfolio.

sections.label: Footer
sections.instruction: This short line appears at the very bottom of your portfolio. It is your final sign-off.

fields.name: text
fields.label: Footer Message
fields.instruction: Write a short closing line. Usually just your name and the year, or a fun personal touch. For example: Made with care by Alex · 2024.
